# GateIo.Net Full LLM Context

> Detailed AI/LLM reference for generating correct C#/.NET code with GateIo.Net. Use this file when `llms.txt` is too brief and the task needs concrete examples, endpoint routing guidance, or anti-hallucination rules.

GateIo.Net is a strongly typed .NET client library for the Gate.io REST and WebSocket APIs. It is part of the CryptoExchange.Net ecosystem and uses the same result, shared API, logging, rate limiting, and socket patterns as the other JKorf exchange libraries.

Current package version in this repository: 3.10.2.
CryptoExchange.Net dependency: 11.1.0.
Target frameworks: netstandard2.0, netstandard2.1, net8.0, net9.0, net10.0.
Native AOT: supported on compatible .NET targets.

Primary documentation:
- README.md: overview, installation, feature table, release notes, basic examples
- docs site: https://cryptoexchange.jkorf.dev/GateIo.Net/
- API quick map: `docs/ai-api-map.md`
- source interfaces: `GateIo.Net/Interfaces/Clients/**`
- AI examples: `Examples/ai-friendly/`

## Golden Rules For AI Code Generation

- Use `GateIoRestClient` for REST and `GateIoSocketClient` for WebSocket. Do not generate raw `HttpClient` calls to Gate.io endpoints.
- Use `GateIoCredentials`, not generic `ApiCredentials`, for API key setup.
- Include `using GateIo.Net;` when using `GateIoCredentials` or `GateIoEnvironment`.
- Always check `.Success` before reading `.Data`.
- REST methods return `WebCallResult<T>` or `WebCallResult`.
- WebSocket subscriptions and socket API requests return `CallResult<T>` or `CallResult`.
- Reuse clients. Do not instantiate a REST or socket client per request in production code.
- Prefer dependency injection for long-running services.
- Store WebSocket subscriptions and unsubscribe during shutdown.
- Do not call `.Result` or `.Wait()` on async methods.
- Do not invent endpoint names. If unsure, inspect `GateIo.Net/Interfaces/Clients/**`.
- Use underscore symbols such as `BTC_USDT` for native Gate.io methods.
- Include a settlement asset such as `"usdt"` for `PerpetualFuturesApi` methods.
- For futures orders, quantity is an `int` number of contracts. Inspect contract metadata before sizing.
- For multi-exchange code, use `CryptoExchange.Net.SharedApis` interfaces from each exchange's `.SharedClient`.

## Installation

```bash
dotnet add package GateIo.Net
```

## Required Usings

Typical REST/trading code:

```csharp
using GateIo.Net;
using GateIo.Net.Clients;
using GateIo.Net.Enums;
using GateIo.Net.Objects;
```

Shared API code:

```csharp
using GateIo.Net.Clients;
using CryptoExchange.Net.SharedApis;
```

Retry/error helper code may also need:

```csharp
using CryptoExchange.Net.Objects;
```

Dependency injection setup:

```csharp
using GateIo.Net;
using Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection;
```

## Client Setup

Public market data does not require credentials:

```csharp
var client = new GateIoRestClient();

var ticker = await client.SpotApi.ExchangeData.GetTickersAsync("ETH_USDT");
if (!ticker.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine(ticker.Error);
    return;
}

Console.WriteLine(ticker.Data.First().LastPrice);
```

Private account and trading endpoints require credentials:

```csharp
var client = new GateIoRestClient(options =>
{
    options.ApiCredentials = new GateIoCredentials("API_KEY", "API_SECRET");
});
```

Socket client with credentials:

```csharp
var socketClient = new GateIoSocketClient(options =>
{
    options.ApiCredentials = new GateIoCredentials("API_KEY", "API_SECRET");
});
```

Environments:

```csharp
var live = new GateIoRestClient(options => options.Environment = GateIoEnvironment.Live);
var demo = new GateIoRestClient(options => options.Environment = GateIoEnvironment.Demo);
```

## Dependency Injection

Current DI shape:

```csharp
services.AddGateIo(options =>
{
    options.Rest.ApiCredentials = new GateIoCredentials("API_KEY", "API_SECRET");
    options.Socket.ApiCredentials = new GateIoCredentials("API_KEY", "API_SECRET");
});
```

Inject:

```csharp
public class MyService
{
    private readonly IGateIoRestClient _restClient;
    private readonly IGateIoSocketClient _socketClient;

    public MyService(IGateIoRestClient restClient, IGateIoSocketClient socketClient)
    {
        _restClient = restClient;
        _socketClient = socketClient;
    }
}
```

Interface namespaces:

```csharp
using GateIo.Net.Interfaces.Clients;
```

## Result Handling Pattern

Always branch on `.Success`.

```csharp
var result = await client.SpotApi.ExchangeData.GetTickersAsync("ETH_USDT");
if (!result.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine($"Code: {result.Error?.Code}");
    Console.WriteLine($"Message: {result.Error?.Message}");
    return;
}

var price = result.Data.First().LastPrice;
```

For retry logic, only retry transient errors:

```csharp
async Task<WebCallResult<T>> WithRetry<T>(Func<Task<WebCallResult<T>>> call, int maxAttempts = 3)
{
    WebCallResult<T> last = default!;

    for (var attempt = 1; attempt <= maxAttempts; attempt++)
    {
        last = await call();
        if (last.Success)
            return last;

        if (last.Error?.IsTransient != true)
            return last;

        await Task.Delay(TimeSpan.FromMilliseconds(250 * Math.Pow(2, attempt)));
    }

    return last;
}
```

## Main API Surface

REST:

```text
GateIoRestClient
  .SpotApi
    .ExchangeData
    .Account
    .Trading
    .SharedClient
  .PerpetualFuturesApi
    .ExchangeData
    .Account
    .Trading
    .SharedClient
  .AlphaApi
    .ExchangeData
    .Account
    .Trading
  .RebateApi
    .Partner
```

WebSocket:

```text
GateIoSocketClient
  .SpotApi
    .SharedClient
    SubscribeToTickerUpdatesAsync
    SubscribeToKlineUpdatesAsync
    SubscribeToOrderBookUpdatesAsync
    SubscribeToOrderUpdatesAsync
    PlaceOrderAsync / CancelOrderAsync / GetOrderAsync
  .PerpetualFuturesApi
    .SharedClient
    SubscribeToTickerUpdatesAsync
    SubscribeToKlineUpdatesAsync
    SubscribeToPositionUpdatesAsync
    SubscribeToOrderUpdatesAsync
    PlaceOrderAsync / CancelOrderAsync / GetOrderAsync
```

Important: GateIo.Net does not have Binance-style `UsdFuturesApi` or `CoinFuturesApi` roots. Use `PerpetualFuturesApi` and pass the settlement asset, for example `"usdt"`.

## Intent To Method Map

For a broader quick reference, see `docs/ai-api-map.md`.

Common REST tasks:

```text
Get spot ticker -> client.SpotApi.ExchangeData.GetTickersAsync("ETH_USDT")
Get all spot tickers -> client.SpotApi.ExchangeData.GetTickersAsync()
Get spot symbol info -> client.SpotApi.ExchangeData.GetSymbolAsync("ETH_USDT")
Get all spot symbols -> client.SpotApi.ExchangeData.GetSymbolsAsync()
Get spot balances -> client.SpotApi.Account.GetBalancesAsync()
Get deposits -> client.SpotApi.Account.GetDepositsAsync()
Get withdrawals -> client.SpotApi.Account.GetWithdrawalsAsync()
Place spot order -> client.SpotApi.Trading.PlaceOrderAsync(...)
Get spot order -> client.SpotApi.Trading.GetOrderAsync(symbol, orderId)
Cancel spot order -> client.SpotApi.Trading.CancelOrderAsync(symbol, orderId)
Place spot trigger order -> client.SpotApi.Trading.PlaceTriggerOrderAsync(...)
Get futures contracts -> client.PerpetualFuturesApi.ExchangeData.GetContractsAsync("usdt")
Get futures ticker -> client.PerpetualFuturesApi.ExchangeData.GetTickersAsync("usdt", "ETH_USDT")
Get futures position -> client.PerpetualFuturesApi.Trading.GetPositionAsync("usdt", "ETH_USDT")
Set futures leverage -> client.PerpetualFuturesApi.Trading.UpdatePositionLeverageAsync("usdt", "ETH_USDT", leverage)
Place futures order -> client.PerpetualFuturesApi.Trading.PlaceOrderAsync(...)
Alpha tickers -> client.AlphaApi.ExchangeData.GetTickersAsync()
Rebate partner subordinates -> client.RebateApi.Partner.GetSubordinatesAsync()
```

Common WebSocket tasks:

```text
Subscribe spot ticker -> socketClient.SpotApi.SubscribeToTickerUpdatesAsync(symbol, handler)
Subscribe spot klines -> socketClient.SpotApi.SubscribeToKlineUpdatesAsync(symbol, interval, handler)
Subscribe spot order book -> socketClient.SpotApi.SubscribeToOrderBookUpdatesAsync(symbol, handler)
Subscribe spot authenticated orders -> socketClient.SpotApi.SubscribeToOrderUpdatesAsync(handler)
Subscribe futures ticker -> socketClient.PerpetualFuturesApi.SubscribeToTickerUpdatesAsync(settle, contract, handler)
Subscribe futures klines -> socketClient.PerpetualFuturesApi.SubscribeToKlineUpdatesAsync(settle, contract, interval, handler)
Subscribe futures positions -> socketClient.PerpetualFuturesApi.SubscribeToPositionUpdatesAsync(userId, settle, handler)
Socket API spot place order -> socketClient.SpotApi.PlaceOrderAsync(...)
Socket API futures place order -> socketClient.PerpetualFuturesApi.PlaceOrderAsync(...)
```

## Spot Order Example

```csharp
using GateIo.Net;
using GateIo.Net.Clients;
using GateIo.Net.Enums;
using GateIo.Net.Objects;

var client = new GateIoRestClient(options =>
{
    options.ApiCredentials = new GateIoCredentials("API_KEY", "API_SECRET");
});

var order = await client.SpotApi.Trading.PlaceOrderAsync(
    symbol: "ETH_USDT",
    side: OrderSide.Buy,
    type: NewOrderType.Limit,
    quantity: 0.01m,
    price: 2000m,
    timeInForce: TimeInForce.GoodTillCancel);

if (!order.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine(order.Error);
    return;
}

Console.WriteLine(order.Data.Id);
```

For market buy orders, Gate.io uses quote currency quantity:

```csharp
var order = await client.SpotApi.Trading.PlaceOrderAsync(
    symbol: "ETH_USDT",
    side: OrderSide.Buy,
    type: NewOrderType.Market,
    quantity: 100m);
```

## Perpetual Futures Order Example

```csharp
using GateIo.Net;
using GateIo.Net.Clients;
using GateIo.Net.Enums;
using GateIo.Net.Objects;

var client = new GateIoRestClient(options =>
{
    options.ApiCredentials = new GateIoCredentials("API_KEY", "API_SECRET");
});

const string settle = "usdt";
const string contract = "ETH_USDT";

var leverage = await client.PerpetualFuturesApi.Trading.UpdatePositionLeverageAsync(settle, contract, 5);
if (!leverage.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine(leverage.Error);
    return;
}

var order = await client.PerpetualFuturesApi.Trading.PlaceOrderAsync(
    settlementAsset: settle,
    contract: contract,
    orderSide: OrderSide.Buy,
    quantity: 1,
    price: 0m,
    timeInForce: TimeInForce.ImmediateOrCancel);

if (!order.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine(order.Error);
    return;
}
```

Use `reduceOnly: true` or `closePosition: true` when closing a futures position if appropriate for the strategy. For dual-mode positions, use `closeSide`.

## WebSocket Subscription Example

```csharp
using GateIo.Net.Clients;
using GateIo.Net.Enums;

var socketClient = new GateIoSocketClient();

var tickerSub = await socketClient.SpotApi.SubscribeToTickerUpdatesAsync(
    "ETH_USDT",
    update => Console.WriteLine(update.Data.LastPrice));

if (!tickerSub.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine(tickerSub.Error);
    return;
}

Console.ReadLine();

await socketClient.UnsubscribeAsync(tickerSub.Data);
```

Multiple subscriptions should all be checked before `.Data` is used.

```csharp
var klineSub = await socketClient.SpotApi.SubscribeToKlineUpdatesAsync(
    "ETH_USDT",
    KlineInterval.OneMinute,
    update =>
    {
        if (update.Data.Final)
            Console.WriteLine(update.Data.ClosePrice);
    });

if (!klineSub.Success)
{
    await socketClient.UnsubscribeAsync(tickerSub.Data);
    Console.WriteLine(klineSub.Error);
    return;
}
```

## SharedApis Multi-Exchange Pattern

Use CryptoExchange.Net.SharedApis when the user asks for code that can work against GateIo and other exchanges.

```csharp
using GateIo.Net.Clients;
using CryptoExchange.Net.SharedApis;

ISpotTickerRestClient tickerClient = new GateIoRestClient().SpotApi.SharedClient;
var symbol = new SharedSymbol(TradingMode.Spot, "ETH", "USDT");

var ticker = await tickerClient.GetSpotTickerAsync(new GetTickerRequest(symbol));
if (!ticker.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine(ticker.Error);
    return;
}

Console.WriteLine(ticker.Data.LastPrice);
```

Shared WebSocket subscriptions return an `UpdateSubscription` in `.Data`. For shared interface variables, unsubscribe through the concrete socket client:

```csharp
var socketClient = new GateIoSocketClient();
ITickerSocketClient tickerSocket = socketClient.SpotApi.SharedClient;

var sub = await tickerSocket.SubscribeToTickerUpdatesAsync(
    new SubscribeTickerRequest(new SharedSymbol(TradingMode.Spot, "ETH", "USDT")),
    update => Console.WriteLine(update.Data.LastPrice));

if (!sub.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine(sub.Error);
    return;
}

Console.ReadLine();

await socketClient.UnsubscribeAsync(sub.Data);
```

Do not call `tickerSocket.UnsubscribeAsync(...)` on `ITickerSocketClient`; that shared interface does not expose the method.

## Symbol And Sizing Handling

Use source models instead of guessing. For spot symbol metadata:

```csharp
var symbolInfo = await client.SpotApi.ExchangeData.GetSymbolAsync("ETH_USDT");
if (!symbolInfo.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine(symbolInfo.Error);
    return;
}
```

For futures contract metadata:

```csharp
var contractInfo = await client.PerpetualFuturesApi.ExchangeData.GetContractAsync("usdt", "ETH_USDT");
if (!contractInfo.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine(contractInfo.Error);
    return;
}

Console.WriteLine($"Multiplier: {contractInfo.Data.Multiplier}");
```

Futures order quantity is an integer number of contracts. Do not pass decimal base asset amounts.

## Error Handling Notes

GateIo.Net returns API, network, and rate-limit errors through `.Error` on result objects. It generally does not throw for normal API failures. Exceptions are for misconfiguration, disposal, cancellation, or programming errors.

Common Gate.io mistake categories:

```text
Invalid symbol: using Binance-style ETHUSDT instead of Gate.io ETH_USDT.
Missing settlement asset: calling PerpetualFuturesApi methods without "usdt", "btc", or "usd".
Invalid futures quantity: passing decimal base amount instead of int contract count.
Missing credentials: calling private account/trading endpoints without GateIoCredentials.
Permanent order validation failure: price, quantity, time-in-force, account type, or leverage does not match symbol/contract limits.
Transient transport/rate-limit failure: retry only if result.Error?.IsTransient == true.
```

## AI-Friendly Examples

Use these examples when generating code:

```text
Examples/ai-friendly/01-spot-quickstart.cs
  Client setup, public ticker, authenticated balances, limit spot order, order status, cancellation.

Examples/ai-friendly/02-futures.cs
  Perpetual futures settlement asset, contract metadata, leverage, order placement, position lookup, reduce-only close.

Examples/ai-friendly/03-websocket.cs
  Public spot ticker stream, spot kline stream, authenticated spot order/balance streams, futures public ticker stream, success checks, teardown.

Examples/ai-friendly/04-multi-exchange.cs
  CryptoExchange.Net.SharedApis REST and socket patterns.

Examples/ai-friendly/05-error-handling.cs
  WebCallResult pattern, transient retry, Gate.io symbol/futures routing mistakes, metadata checks.
```

These files are intended to be copied into a console `Program.cs` after `dotnet add package GateIo.Net`.

## Anti-Hallucination Checklist

Before finalizing generated code:

- Does it compile with the current package?
- Does it include `using GateIo.Net;` when using credentials or environments?
- Does every REST/socket result check `.Success` before `.Data`?
- Does it use the correct API branch: `SpotApi`, `PerpetualFuturesApi`, `AlphaApi`, or `RebateApi`?
- Does it avoid raw Gate.io URLs and manual signing?
- Does it avoid legacy `GateIoClient`?
- Does native Gate.io code use underscore symbols such as `ETH_USDT`?
- Do futures calls include the settlement asset?
- Do futures orders use an `int` contract quantity?
- Does it avoid `.Result` and `.Wait()`?
- Are WebSocket subscriptions unsubscribed on shutdown?
- For shared socket interfaces, does it keep the concrete socket client for `UnsubscribeAsync(sub.Data)`?

## Source Of Truth For Method Names

When unsure, inspect these files instead of guessing:

```text
GateIo.Net/Interfaces/Clients/IGateIoRestClient.cs
GateIo.Net/Interfaces/Clients/IGateIoSocketClient.cs
GateIo.Net/Interfaces/Clients/SpotApi/IGateIoRestClientSpotApi*.cs
GateIo.Net/Interfaces/Clients/SpotApi/IGateIoSocketClientSpotApi*.cs
GateIo.Net/Interfaces/Clients/PerpetualFuturesApi/IGateIoRestClientPerpetualFuturesApi*.cs
GateIo.Net/Interfaces/Clients/PerpetualFuturesApi/IGateIoSocketClientPerpetualFuturesApi*.cs
GateIo.Net/Interfaces/Clients/AlphaApi/IGateIoRestClientAlphaApi*.cs
GateIo.Net/Interfaces/Clients/RebateApi/IGateIoRestClientRebateApi*.cs
```

Prefer the interface files over memory when generating endpoint names, parameters, callback names, and return types.

## Common Wrong Patterns To Avoid

Wrong:

```csharp
var client = new GateIoClient();
```

Use:

```csharp
var client = new GateIoRestClient();
```

Wrong:

```csharp
options.ApiCredentials = new ApiCredentials("key", "secret");
```

Use:

```csharp
options.ApiCredentials = new GateIoCredentials("key", "secret");
```

Wrong:

```csharp
var result = await client.SpotApi.ExchangeData.GetTickersAsync("ETHUSDT");
Console.WriteLine(result.Data.First().LastPrice);
```

Use:

```csharp
var result = await client.SpotApi.ExchangeData.GetTickersAsync("ETH_USDT");
if (!result.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine(result.Error);
    return;
}
Console.WriteLine(result.Data.First().LastPrice);
```

Wrong:

```csharp
await client.PerpetualFuturesApi.Trading.PlaceOrderAsync("ETH_USDT", OrderSide.Buy, 1);
```

Use:

```csharp
await client.PerpetualFuturesApi.Trading.PlaceOrderAsync(
    settlementAsset: "usdt",
    contract: "ETH_USDT",
    orderSide: OrderSide.Buy,
    quantity: 1);
```

Wrong:

```csharp
await sharedTickerSocket.UnsubscribeAsync(sub.Data);
```

Use:

```csharp
await socketClient.UnsubscribeAsync(sub.Data);
```
